name: High-Severity Residuals Acknowledged Before Commit
description: Before a commit, every high- or critical-severity residual must be acknowledged, addressed, or waived — not left open. A commit must not silently carry an unacknowledged high-severity gap.
formula: G(GitCommit → ∀ r ∈ residuals . r.severity ≥ High → r.status ≠ Open)
rationale: Commits are where work crosses a boundary into the shared history. Requiring high-severity residuals to be explicitly acknowledged before commit ensures a reviewer downstream inherits a triaged residual surface, not a pile of silent open gaps.

examples.passes: A trace with a GitCommit where the one high-severity residual has status 'acknowledged'.
examples.fails: A trace with a GitCommit while a { severity: high, status: open } residual remains.
